name: White Mousse Cake with Zest of Grapefruit
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Pine nuts, chopped or whole
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease, flour and line the bottom of a 15 ½-inch x 10 ½-inch jellyroll pan with wax paper.
2. Beat egg yolks with 3/4 cup sugar until mixture falls in ribbon when beaters are lifted. Stir in 1/4 cup flour and vanilla extract.
3. Beat egg whites until stiff but not dry. Fold into the yolk mixture.
4. Turn batter into prepared pan, spreading evenly. Bake at 350°F for 20 minutes. Cool.
5. To make mousse, combine 1 cup sugar, lemon juice and water in a saucepan. Add citrus peels and cook until tender. Drain peel, reserve syrup for another use.
6. Using half of the chocolate, make chocolate curls with a vegetable peeler. Place curls on a waxed paper-lined tray and freeze to harden.
7. Meanwhile, melt remaining chocolate in the top of a double boiler over barely simmering water. Cool.
8. Whip cream until stiff. Alternately fold in half of the chocolate, half of the peel, and half of the pine nuts into the cream. Add remaining melted chocolate, half of the remaining peel, and half of the remaining pine nuts until incorporated into the cream.
9. Cut genoise crosswise into 4 equal rectangular layers. If using a purchased loaf cake, split into 4 layers.
10. Spread some white chocolate mousse on the first layer. Top with another cake layer. Spread with more mousse. Repeat with the next 2 layers, spreading mousse over top and sides.
11. Cover top and sides with frozen chocolate curls. Delicately place reserved peel and nuts over chocolate curls. Sprinkle with powdered sugar.
